summary: The sync-react workflow failed when attempting to push a new branch to the vercel/next.js repository. The bot account 'nextjs-bot' was denied push access with a 403 error, indicating insufficient permissions or an invalid/expired token.
root_cause: The GitHub bot account 'nextjs-bot' was denied permission to push to vercel/next.js.git, resulting in a 403 HTTP error and git exit code 128. The token or credentials used by nextjs-bot either lack write access to the repository or have expired.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicit permission denial from GitHub remote for the bot account used by the workflow.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: HTTP 403 confirms the authentication token either lacks write permission or is invalid/expired.

evidence.quote: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: The git push command itself failed, confirming the push step as the point of failure.

suggested_fix: Rotate or re-authorize the GitHub token used by 'nextjs-bot' in the repository secrets and ensure it has write (push) permissions to vercel/next.js.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
